summary: No project standards violations found. All three changed files (package.json, src/test/setup.ts, PdfViewer.smoke.test.tsx) comply with the rules in CLAUDE.md, automation.md (universal), and the applicable path-specific test rules.

standards_audited.role: path-specific — did NOT load (file paths do not match globs: tests/**/*.spec.ts, tests/**/*.test.ts, playwright.config.ts, tests/support/**/*.ts, tests/utils/**/*.ts)
standards_audited.note: Smoke test is at src/app/components/figma/PdfViewer/__tests__/ — outside the tests/ directory globs. Rules in this file do not govern the changed files.

standards_audited.role: path-specific — did NOT load (same glob mismatch as test-patterns.md)

rules_checked: CLAUDE.md: Development Commands (no dependency pinning rules)
file_assessments.result: No violation. CLAUDE.md does not prescribe dependency pinning strategy.

file_assessments.change: Added DOMMatrix polyfill (~40 lines) with identity matrix, CSS parsing, and stub methods
file_assessments.result: No violation. Follows existing polyfill pattern (ResizeObserver, IntersectionObserver, localStorage, matchMedia). Well-documented with explanatory comments. No hardcoded colors, no Date.now() usage.

file_assessments.change: New 26-line smoke test verifying pdf.js worker setup and error fallback rendering
rules_checked: CLAUDE.md: File Structure (valid location under src/app/components/figma/), Key Conventions
automation.md: All 8 ESLint rules — no hardcoded colors, no Date.now(), no waitForTimeout, no manual IndexedDB seeding, no inline styles, no silent catches
file_assessments.result: No violation. Test file is in project-standard location. Uses vitest + @testing-library/react pattern consistent with existing component tests. Deep relative import (../../../../../lib/pdfWorker) is valid but could use @/lib/pdfWorker — however CLAUDE.md does not mandate @ alias usage, so this is not a standards violation.

notes: The smoke test file location (src/app/components/figma/PdfViewer/__tests__/) is consistent with the project's existing pattern for multi-file component directories (cf. src/stores/__tests__/).

Path-specific test rules (test-patterns.md, test-cleanup.md) apply only to files under tests/** — they do not govern co-located unit tests in src/. This is by design per the path frontmatter in those rule files.

The deep relative import '../../../../../lib/pdfWorker' in the smoke test resolves correctly to src/lib/pdfWorker.ts. Using @/lib/pdfWorker would be more robust against file moves, but CLAUDE.md does not mandate alias usage.
